iterations.strategy: Root cause (Category B — Algorithm change): Phase 1 (dim_filter) consumed 11626ms of 12147ms total. The iter_0 plan used per-thread std::unordered_map<int32_t,double> with reserve(1<<20) per thread — this causes 64×8MB=512MB of bucket arrays far exceeding the 44MB L3 cache, ~16M heap-allocated linked-list nodes (one per row processed), a single-threaded merge into a 128MB global hash table, and 16M free() calls during swap(). The fix is to eliminate the hash table entirely by using the pre-built lineitem_orderkey_sorted index for Phase 1: since pairs are already sorted by l_orderkey, groups are contiguous and a sequential scan with a running sum replaces the hash aggregation. Each of the 64 threads advances its chunk boundaries to complete group edges (pairs[start].key != pairs[start-1].key), processes contiguous groups sequentially with random l_quantity[row_id] accesses, and emits hot keys to a thread-local vector — no hash table, no heap allocation, no merge step. Expected Phase 1 runtime: ~480MB sequential index read at 50GB/s + ~480MB random l_quantity reads at 10GB/s with 64 threads = ~55ms total, down from 11626ms. Phase 2 (point lookups for ~3000 hot keys) is unchanged.
